reviews.review_description:
Repetative, boring, slow. No comprehensive exercises and stretches. You can not filter the part of the body you want to exercise you have to search it manually.
Mo medical stretches for the back issues and problems.
Overall, it consumes huge amount of your time and doesn't offer much.
You can not even skip the parts of the exercise you don't need. Worse than a video tutorial.

I love this app, the interface is amazing and the instructors are really great!

I do have one suggestion; it would be great if there were longer sets. I did read that you can select your favourites so you can do a series but I cannot seem to find that ANYWHERE. 

Please add longer sets so we can get a full body stretch. 

Thanks!

developer_response.body:
I'm so glad you are enjoying Stretch Lab! Unfortunately, there isn't a way to favorite. On the Home page, the Programs and Collections section includes Beginner's Series, Total Body, Lower Body, and Upper Body classes. Have you tried this out yet?

Thank you for taking the time to leave a review and share your feedback!

Thank you.

Joyce
Litesport

reviews.review_description: So far so good. I like the guided stretching as well as the instructor view is in multiple places around you making it easy to turn or rotate without losing the instructor. I wish there was a way to favourite certain videos or search by a specific area you’re looking to target, or build a playlist of multiple stretches that you need to do daily.

developer_response.body:
All good suggestions. Thank you for the feedback!

Joyce
Litesport

reviews.review_description: This has became my favourite app. It's so easy to follow the coach, because you can see she/he where ever you turn your head. Great work!

developer_response.body:
We're glad you are enjoying the app! Thank you for taking the time to leave a review. 

Joyce
Litesport

reviews.review_description: I specifically downloaded it for neck and back / posture excersizes and theres only 3 videos from each trainer and they all copied each-others stretches so its almost the same videos different person, seemed like a massive waste of money considering you cant even favorite stretches or make a playlist so you can go in every day and do the same playlist etc.
It’s a great app when it works but I’d get 10 minutes into a stretch and the instructor will disappear and all I can hear is their voice. Waste of time.

developer_response.body:
I'm sorry to hear you are having a frustrating experience with the Stretch Lab application. Does the loss of video happen frequently? Do you see any changes in video quality before this happens?

I would be happy to help resolve this issue, but I need additional information. Please feel free to contact me at support@litesport.com.

Thank you.

Joyce
Litesport

reviews.review_description: I think it helps to have a "coach" guiding me on stretches. I sit in front of a computer all day long and taking a couple of breaks througout the day has really helped. Having a coach seems to motivate me to do the streaching, and their are different sessions for different muscle groups making it easy to focus on any areas that may need more attention.

reviews.review_title: I use only three apps in VR...
reviews.review_description:
and one of the apps I use daily is Stretch LAB—twice a day, just after my TRIPP session and before the ACEXR daily drills.

The app offers a wide variety of pre-recorded guided stretching sessions, with a clear division into different parts of the body. You can target any area you want with sessions lasting 5, 10, or 20 minutes. Wherever you look, you’ll see a virtual coach who explains everything clearly and slowly, ensuring you understand every movement you’re practicing. Honestly, no yoga class has ever provided me with this level of explanation. With Stretch LAB, you’re fully aware of every muscle being engaged and can connect with your body on a deeper level. It’s incredible—you’ll laugh with surprise at what your body is capable of! Every day, I smile in disbelief at how much I can feel and improve.

The app works seamlessly in full Mixed Reality, so you can exercise wherever you want while still being able to interact with others. My 3-year-old daughter has even started exercising with me! Sometimes, I cast the app to the TV so everyone can see what’s happening. So far, three other people I know have started using the app because of this!

All you need is a chair, yoga blocks, and a mat—that’s it.

That said, I’d love to see a couple of updates from the developers:
	1.	Breathing Visualization: The breathing cues could be improved. For instance, if it worked like in the TRIPP app, it would be amazing—perhaps making the exhale red and the inhale blue.
	2.	More Filters: Adding additional filters to target specific body parts would make the app even more versatile.

I tweaked my back right before Christmas, and as someone who deals with sciatica issues, I was desperately searching for something to help stretch it out and relieve the pain. That’s when I discovered StretchLab, and it has been nothing short of amazing!

The guided sessions are intuitive, and the trainers really feel like they’re there with you every step of the way. The stretches are thoughtfully designed and have significantly helped me regain mobility and reduce the pain in my lower back. I can’t thank the developers and trainers enough for creating such a life-changing app.

This app has become an essential part of my recovery and daily routine. Highly recommended for anyone dealing with back pain or mobility issues! Thank you for making such an incredible tool for wellness!

I like the idea but the basic stretches are not that basic at all. I dread to think what happens after that. I’ve only had about 17 minutes so far, but that was enough to realise the beginner section is too advanced. The intro video for 16 seconds doesn’t convey the app, what’s needed or what’s needed either. If I’m in VR stretching, I really don’t want to have to go and get a mat, chair and any other accessories. From the title name, I seriously thought I’d learn some stretches and warmups that would be a useful to transition to many other exercise apps I already have. The breathing in the intro seemed relaxing and I really thought this is amazing and would progress from there. Then the next minute I’m stretching to an unnatural position that was impossible. Having looked at the devs other apps, the same constructive criticism applies. There isn’t enough of an explanation or differentiation between them all. I’ve refunded, even though i got this at a great price. It doesn’t do what it says on the tin. 

On the plus side, the presentation seems good, the idea of being able to see the instructor wherever you are is too. Why are they not presented in VR though? That would make the experience so much more immersive. I also really like the wall art too. Finally, why do we have to use hand tracking and not controllers. This app seems like a real missed opportunity, hopefully in time it improves.

Love this app, it's my favorite workout app on VR. I was struggling with a sore back from remote working and this app changed everything. I do a couple different stretch sessions before starting my work day and I feel fantastic!

StretchLab is a fantastic VR app that makes stretching engaging and accessible for everyone. The immersive environment keeps you focused, while the guided exercises ensure proper technique and variety. Whether you’re easing tension after a long day or enhancing flexibility, StretchLab delivers a relaxing yet effective experience. Highly recommended for anyone looking to improve mobility and well-being!

Everybody needs this app! You'll feel great after these stretching sessions. Being able to see the instructor no matter the position I'm in is perfect! I also love the breathing feature and wish you'd add it to all your workout apps! The only thing missing is a list of items needed like yoga blocks and straps. I think people should be aware of this when buying the app.

It is a very interesting app, works well with the instructor seen from all angles. Just hope more sessions are made available and not as paid dlc in the future. As title says it does a job but I feel a few weeks in it is going to get very repetitive.

The developers of this app managed to anticipate all the users' needs and delivered an app that is well polished, picturesque, and intuitive. I really enjoyed my lesson, and I love how the coach appears wherever you look.
I use the app almost daily. An option to turn the music off would be nice so you can play your own music in the background; the same applies to Pilates. Still 5 stars.

I like the trainer and the exercises but I see the guardian and my living room?!? I thought it should be in a studio and when I use VR I want to be in another environment than my living room. And since I have a Quest 2 it’s in black and white! Is it supposed to be like this?

developer_response.body:
When you select a workout, there is a toggle above the description to switch between Studio MR (Mixed Reality) and Full MR. If you select Studio MR, the top half of your view will show the studio. The bottom half will be passthrough and black and white with the Quest 2. This lets you see the mat, your feet, and any equipment you use during the workout. You can also pause a workout and switch between Studio MR and Full MR.

I hope this helps!

Joyce

reviews.review_description:
Edit: I have no idea why the developer responded to my review with instructions on mixed reality as my review did not report problems with that. Hopefully they will read through the full reviews and extract the meat of the feedback we are providing and use it to shape the game.

------
Love the potential but the trailers provide no real info about how this program works. I almost did not buy this due to the lack of basic info available. 

This is not an immersive experience in VR. It is an MR experience that brings a 2D trainer into your room and some interactive elements depending on the program. I am fine with that but you should know what you are getting. In the Stretch workout that I did, there was no feedback or assessment if what I was doing was correct and nothing interactive. The biggest plus of this program is that the trainer is always in view from every angle. No neck craning like you would at a TV. 

You are essentially on your own to sort, filter and choose what you want to do. There is no onboarding that takes your height, weight, level of activity and curates a program for you so it can be daunting for someone that is starting a path to fitness and needs guidance. The short Stretch program I chose appeared to be beginner oriented but opened with a 60 second hold on a very deep squat that I barely survived. 

I like the one time purchase of the program rather than a subscription. I wish they would have included their Yoga program in this. Hopefully that will be released at some point.

I'll review each of the 5 apps as I try them.

developer_response.body:
Hi cvan65!

Replying to your review again to address your specific concerns here. Thanks again for taking time to leave a review for StretchLabs! We're sorry to hear of the confusion from the trailer and will let our team know. There is more information about the app in the description page here: https://www.meta.com/experiences/stretchlab/7457571521021518/

Regarding a more beginner friendly class, you may want to start with the 101 beginner series of classes first! We will also forward your feedback for more user-specific onboarding and curated programs for future consideration! 

We're glad you appreciated the one-time purchase and hope you enjoy trying out all 5 apps. We look forward to hearing any other feedback or comments. Thanks again for taking time to let us know these great suggestions.

Feel free to reach out to us any time easily here: https://support.litesport.com/hc/en-us/requests/new

reviews.review_description: Clicked on workouts waited for minutes and the program just spins nothing loads

developer_response.body:
Updated Response:
Hello BattlestarPegasus,

Thank you again for taking time to leave your feedback regarding the issue you experienced loading some of the StretchLab classes. Our team identified an issue causing some of the workouts not to load that has now been resolved. We apologize for any inconvenience; as of November 18, 2024 at 5:15 pm EST, the issue has been fixed and all workouts should open as expected. Should you have any other questions, concerns, or feedback regarding this fix please don’t hesitate to reach back out to our development team at the link below: 

https://support.litesport.com/hc/en-us/requests/new

Thanks again and enjoy your workouts with StretchLabs!

reviews.review_description: Great way to get stretching, it would really helpful if there were a few more options. E.G. Hip stretches for people with knee problems would be really 👍
